task: Task 14 — Post diff inline comments to GitHub PR
status: DONE — backend IPC + UI button + store action + validation tests + build clean
files_changed: src-tauri/src/lib.rs — new github_pr_post_diff_comment IPC + validate_post_diff_comment_args + head_sha added to GhPrDto at both gh_pr_view sites
src/lib/types.ts — head_sha added to GitHubPullRequest interface
src/components/workspace/DiffCommentPopover.tsx — Post to GitHub button per unposted comment + Posted badge + no-PR hint

gh_payload_shape.owner_repo_discovery: gh repo view --json owner,nameWithOwner (parsed in Rust; nameWithOwner is already 'owner/name' so we use it directly instead of re-joining owner.login + name)

args.note: raw field so it ships as JSON int, not string

gh_payload_shape.response_parsing: serde_json::from_str(stdout) -> .get('id').and_then(.as_u64) -> error if missing, 0, or non-uint
gh_payload_shape.error_contract: gh_unavailable / gh_unauthenticated / rate_limited / gh_error (via classify_gh_error, same as the rest of the gh surface)

store_action.name: markDiffCommentPosted
store_action.signature: (wsId: string, commentId: string, remoteId: number, postedAt: string) => void
store_action.behavior: Finds the comment in diffComments[wsId] and stamps remote_id + posted_at immutably. Idempotent — if already stamped with the same values, no-op (returns s unchanged so React doesn't re-render).
store_action.duplicate_post_guard: The render layer hides the 'Post to GitHub' button when c.remote_id is truthy. The action itself also early-returns in the function body for defense in depth.

ui_button.location: Per comment in the DiffCommentPopover, alongside Edit + Delete
ui_button.hide_when: c.remote_id is set (Posted badge shows instead)
ui_button.disabled_state: no PR (hint 'No PR for this branch' shows at popover bottom) OR pr.head_sha is null (hint 'PR data is missing the head commit SHA. Refresh the Checks tab to fetch it.')
ui_button.confirm: useUI.askConfirm with title 'Post comment to PR #N?' + confirmLabel 'Post to GitHub'
ui_button.error_toast_kind_mapping: gh_unavailable / gh_unauthenticated -> info; anything else -> error (same as Merge button, Task 13)
ui_button.posted_badge_link: pr.html_url + '#discussion_r' + remote_id (GitHub's standard deep-link format for review comments)

verification.cargo_test_lib: test result: ok. 94 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored (93 pre-existing + 1 new validate_post_diff_comment_args_accepts_valid_inputs)
verification.npm_run_build: tsc -b && vite build — clean. 1812 modules transformed. ✓ built in 444ms.

tests.name: validate_post_diff_comment_args_accepts_valid_inputs
covers: commit_id length negative: empty / 3 chars / 39 chars / 64 chars (SHA-256 length)
error_message_contract: side: starts with 'Invalid side:' AND contains ''left'' AND ''right''
line: contains 'Line must be greater than 0'
commit_id length: contains 'Invalid commit_id length' AND '40 hex chars'
commit_id charset: contains 'Invalid commit_id:' AND '40 hex chars'

cross_task_dependencies: Reuses EMPTY_PR_CHECKS selector + prChecks slice from Task 8
Reuses EMPTY_DIFF_COMMENTS selector + diffComments slice from Task 11
Additive: GitHubPullRequest.head_sha (was null on the wire before; now optional)
Additive: new useWorkspace(wsId) selector (no consumer in the file before)
patterns: snake_case on BOTH sides of the wire (Rust DTO field head_ref_oid ↔ TS field head_sha via the GitHubPullRequest interface; serde default + Option for the gh-version optionality)
async fn + spawn_blocking for the two-gh-subprocess flow (repo view + api POST)
Reuse classify_gh_error for the stable <code>: <message> error prefix
Stable Zustand selectors with frozen empty constants (the new useWorkspace returns the same reference as long as the workspace row doesn't change; falls back to null for absent wsId)
spellCheck={false} autoCorrect='off' autoCapitalize='off' autoComplete='off' is preserved on the textarea (existing)
Tip + onClick={e => { if (!pr) e.preventDefault(); }} on the Posted badge so the link is a no-op when no PR anchor is available
data-testid attributes on every interactive element: diff-comment-post-<id>, diff-comment-posted-<id>, diff-comment-no-pr-hint

qa_scenarios.post_one_inline_comment: Verified by code trace: popover open with pr + head_sha + !c.remote_id → button visible. Click → askConfirm → githubPrPostDiffComment IPC → on success, markDiffCommentPosted(wsId, c.id, remoteId, new Date().toISOString()) → pushToast(`Comment posted to PR #${pr.number}`, 'success'). The store mutation flips the comment's remote_id to a truthy number → selector re-runs → JSX maps to the Posted branch (button hidden, badge + link shown).
qa_scenarios.retry_does_not_duplicate: Verified by code trace: popover open with c.remote_id set → the canPost flag (const canPost = !c.remote_id && !!pr && !!pr.head_sha) is false → button is not rendered. The Posted badge is the only thing in the footer. postToGitHub(c) also early-returns on c.remote_id (defense in depth). No IPC call can be made from the UI for a posted comment.
